commonIssues:
A major issue is the lack of a complete, unbroken chain of ownership (chain of deeds) for older or ancestral properties.

How to fix:

  • Obtain certified copies of previous sale deeds from the Delhi Archives or the respective Sub-Registrar Office.

Discrepancies in property details, such as mismatches in names, address errors, or incorrect plot numbers between documents, often lead to rejection.

How to fix:

  • Prepare a rectification deed if errors are in the sale deed, and submit it to the SRO.

Purchasing property without proper legal due diligence often reveals hidden disputes, encumbrances, or pending litigations later.

How to fix:

  • Conduct a thorough search via DORIS Delhi and obtain a Non-Encumbrance Certificate. DORIS Delhi

Many properties in Delhi are listed as "booked" (marked for demolition) by local authorities (MCD) or falling under prohibited categories, which prevents legal registration.

How to fix:

  • Check MCD's booked property list. Do not purchase until the unauthorized construction is regularised or cleared. MCD Portal

Undervaluation of property to save on taxes is a frequent mistake that leads to severe penalties, as the government charges 2% monthly interest on the deficit amount.

How to fix:

  • Use the online e-valuation tool on the DORIS portal to calculate exact stamp duty before payment.

Despite the shift to digital (DORIS/IGRS), physical verification at the sub-registrar office is still required. Appointments are often hard to get, and wait times are long.

How to fix:

  • Book online appointments in advance through the Delhi Revenue Department portal.

While the registration happens, receiving the final, physically stamped registered deed can take weeks (15 to 30 days or more) due to high workload.

How to fix:

  • Track application status online and retain the registration receipt as proof until delivery.

Many buyers register the sale deed but fail to complete the mutation in the local municipal records (MCD/DDA), leading to problems with utility bills and taxes.

How to fix:

  • Apply for mutation at MCD online portal immediately after registration. MCD Mutation

Sometimes, even after registration, the property records do not reflect accurately in the EC, which can cause trouble when attempting to sell the property or take a loan.

How to fix:

  • Apply for manual EC rectification at the SRO if digital data is missing.

Residents in unauthorised colonies face issues with the PM-UDAY regularisation process, including complexities in documentation, boundary disputes, and high fees.

How to fix:

  • Apply for regularisation through the DDA PM-UDAY portal with required GIS mapping. PM-UDAY Portal

Properties that remain outside the formal registration system are often effectively frozen—they cannot be sold legally, used as loan collateral, or renovated legally.

How to fix:

  • Ensure the property is legally registered and avoid transactions based solely on General Power of Attorney (GPA).
